items.title: The NTE Starter Pack DLC is now available!
items.summary: Hey Appraisers, The Starter Pack DLC is now available! Grab it for a head start in Hethereau! 📦Pack Contents: De-noise Solution x1 Elite Hunter Guide x5 Chaotic Dye x5 Manhole Boss x5 Head to NTE's Steam store page now and find the Starter
items.content:
Hey Appraisers,

The Starter Pack DLC is now available! Grab it for a head start in Hethereau!

📦Pack Contents:

De-noise Solution x1

Elite Hunter Guide x5

Chaotic Dye x5

Manhole Boss x5

Head to NTE's Steam store page now and find the Starter Pack in the DLC section to buy it directly. Once your purchase is complete, the contents are sent automatically to the NTE game account linked to your Steam account, ready to claim and use in-game.

items.title: NTE: Neverness to Everness Has Some Trouble On Steam Deck
items.summary: NTE: Neverness to Everness may be on Steam now, but it falls into the same performance issues on Steam Deck that most similar gacha games encounter.
items.content:
Game Settings Nintendo Reviews News Tips and Guides Other Reviews Accessory Reviews Handheld Reviews About Us jQuery('#-pro-menu-237-7 .oxy-pro-menu-show-dropdown .menu-item-has-children > a', 'body').each(function(){ jQuery(this).append(' '); }); jQuery('#-pro-menu-237-7 .oxy-pro-menu-show-dropdown .menu-item:not(.menu-item-has-children) > a', 'body').each(function(){ jQuery(this).append(' '); }); Search for: Opinion

If you haven't yet, follow us on X (Twitter) , Mastodon , or Bluesky to know when we post new articles, and join our Reddit or Subscribe to us on YouTube to check out our content and interact with our awesome community. Thank you for supporting us!

I’m not sure why, but I’ve been on a gacha game kick lately. I got back into Wuthering Waves and found myself turning my AYN Odin 3 into a Gacha game machine. I downloaded Genshin Impact, Honkai Star Rail, Zenless Zone Zero, and a new one I’ve been playing more of, NTE: Neverness to Everness . It’s been a lot of fun to play, and it was perfect timing because the game just recently launched on Steam. With that in mind, I wanted to check it out and see how the game is running on the Steam Deck and how the experience compares to the standalone version that we can manually install via the game’s launcher.

For those who may not know, NTE: Neverness to Everness is a gacha game created by Hotta Studio. We run around in an urban open-world city, taking on missions, getting into flashy combat, and collecting new characters through its gacha systems. There’s a lot more to it that makes me think of GTA or Marvel’s Spider-Man world than we can get cars to drive around the city quickly or fast travel. It seems very F2P-friendly as well, with lots of unlockables for free and a lot of chances to get the characters we want. Just recently, with the 1.2 update, the team gave out a free character and about 2200 of their premium currency, which is just a treat.

However, performance on the Steam Deck isn’t always the greatest for these kinds of games. A lot of them are very CPU-heavy, which leads to a good chunk of stutters that can harm the experience. Almost every gacha game I’ve played has similar problems, and that includes NTE .

In contained fights, on the lowest possible settings, it can stick to 30 FPS for the most part, though it does stutter and drop here and there when a lot of flashy effects are on the screen. So, some combat scenarios are a bit worse than others. However, running around the city can cost some drops, and driving around is significantly worse. So, I would consider it somewhat playable for minor grinding, but it’s definitely going to have its issues when trying to get around without fast traveling or in large fights.

I also took some time to compare the Steam and Launcher versions of the game. Usually, Steam versions will have slightly better performance and fewer stutters due to the shared shader cache system on Steam. However, it is a fairly new release, so the cache isn’t there yet. Because of this, performance is relatively the same, with the Steam version performing slightly worse. As we can see in the visual below, I tested both games with the same “Smooth” preset on the Fourth Stop of the Gloaming Circle Ghost Train with the same exact characters, and the results are pretty similar to each other.

Ultimately, it’s possible that within the next week, we will see the shader cache appear, and performance will get better, especially when it comes to stutters. However, it’s still going to need those compromises, including lower settings and a 30 FPS cap for stability, and we probably will still have some CPU bottlenecks that result in other stutters, especially when driving around. I do see some possibilities to play on the Steam Deck this way for some of its content, but it’s not something that’ll be stable throughout the entire game.

NTE: Neverness to Everness is currently free to play on Steam right now.

If you enjoyed this article, check out the rest of the content on SteamDeckHQ ! We have a wide variety of game reviews and news that will help your gaming experience. Whether you're looking for news , tips and tutorials , game settings and reviews , or just want to stay up-to-date on the latest trends, we've got you covered!

items.title: New Neverness To Everness (NTE) Update Introduces New RPG Game Mode
items.summary: Neverness To Everness has a new content update that introduces a brand new RPG game mode to the game, 999 Nights. Save the villagers from monsters now.
items.content:
As a huge fan of RPGs, this newest update for Neverness To Everness intrigued me. Update 1.2 introduces the game mode 999 Nights . 999 Nights is a new RPG system that adds tabletop RPG mechanics like classes, gear, and a ton more drops to the game. It is specifically tied to the game mode of 999 Nights .

In 999 Nights , the Appraiser and their friends are whisked away to a fantasy realm: the Warren Continent. It’s home to Fuzzy Village, built by whimsical little creatures called Fuzzies – and it has a dragon problem. The party’s quest: to help the villagers protect their home and slay the evil creature.

In this story, a four-member party consisting of the Appraiser, Mint, Iroi, and Shinku sets out from Fuzzy Village for the grassy plains of Warren. From the plains, they venture into distinct regions – volcano, ice field, and lakelands – battling different monsters along the way before finally reaching the Dragon Keep, where they will challenge the evil Crimson Dragon.

On the Warren Continent, each member of the four-person party has their own class. Defeating monsters earns EXP to level up your characters and boost their combat power, with a chance to receive gear and other kinds of items you can trade at the shop.

New Limited boards: “Before the Dawn” and “The Lifeline” In Version 1.2, Scarborough Fair will run two Limited Boards in sequence: “Before the Dawn” and “The Lifeline”. Roll on them to obtain the limited S-class characters Shinku and Iroi, respectively.

“Before the Dawn” July 8 – 29 Shinku can come across as hard to approach on a day-to-day basis. But when she’s out on a mission, she proves herself dependable far beyond her years. Always the first to charge into danger and shield her teammates, this awkward dragon girl protects the people she cares about. As for social skills…well, she might need a little more time to find her footing there.

Shinku’s Basic Attack unleashes a string of up to five consecutive martial arts attacks that deal Cosmos damage, with careful attack management triggering a variant of her Basic Attack and building up her Defiant Spirit gauge, which lets Shinku enter her Rampage form. Shinku’s ultimate attack then sees her spend all of her Defiant Spirit to enter the Surging Crimson state, launching a multi-stage attack for massive Cosmos damage to the unlucky target.

“The Life Line” July 29 – Aug 19 Warm and approachable, Iroi puts people at ease the moment they meet her, earning their trust before they even realize it. Every pattern of her behavior lines up exactly with what people expect of a “perfectly normal girl”.

Iroi’s Basic Attack is Collaboration, where she summons her lambs for a string of up to five consecutive attacks that deal Anima damage, building Imagination on each hit. Hold Basic Attack and Iroi spends Imagination to charge up, unleashing up to three attacks based on her charge level. Meanwhile, Iroi’s Ultimate sees the gates of dreams swing open. If her Imagination hasn’t reached the required threshold, she opens the Dream of the Horned Gate, providing continuous healing to the whole team. If her Imagination has reached the threshold, she opens the Dream of the Ivory Gate instead, continuously damaging all enemies caught inside.

The Draco Alongside the new characters, a brand-new vehicle pulls up to meet Appraisers: the Draco. The Draco is a ride that breaks all the rules. Structurally, it’s nothing like a traditional motorcycle – it can strafe sideways, and while riding, your Appraiser can even make the whole frame lift clear off the ground! Aside from the player character, Shinku can also ride the Draco, too – with the bike taking on a unique appearance while she wears her “Dragonhunter” outfit.

The Draco is grabbed in the same way the Porsche was grabbed in the last update, the mystery box. You can use fons or annulith to nab mystery boxes and hope for a good pull. I was able to pull the Porsche in the last update, though I don’t know about the Draco this time around. It looks great, but man, does pulling on that mystery box use up a lot of resources.

Hiders can also plant a decoy at a given spot to fool the Hunters, while Hunters have a scan skill that can detect nearby Hiders and decoys. If the Hunters take down every Hider with their attacks before the time runs out, they win. On the flip side, if enough Hiders survive to the end, or every Hunter gets eliminated, the Hiders take the match.

In this mode, Appraisers can invite character companions to join them at the auction, and each one lends a hand in a different way. Every lot up for auction differs in type (value, size, etc), with your companions revealing clues and helping you judge exactly when to make your move.

items.title: Neverness to Everness Available on Steam, Epic Games Store, Galaxy Store
items.summary: Free-to-play RPG Neverness to Everness is available through additional storefronts. The game -- which has also receive its version 1.2 update -- can now be found on Steam, the Epic Games Store, and the Samsung Galaxy Store.
items.content:
by Alex Fuller · Published July 8, 2026 · Updated July 8, 2026

Perfect World and Hotta Studio have made free-to-play RPG Neverness to Everness available through more storefronts. The game — available on PC, iOS, and Android — can now be downloaded via Steam , the Epic Games Store , and the Samsung Galaxy Store (country-dependent). The news comes alongside the release of the game’s Version 1.2 update, titled “999 Nights”.

Neverness to Everness released earlier this year and is set in a world where humanity exists alongside supernatural anomalies. Players use characters’ powerful Esper abilities to navigate and tackle unexpected anomalies. In addition to exploration and combat, players can also modify sports cars, manage businesses, and more. The game includes a day-night cycle with dynamic weather that can impact vehicle handling, with players also able to traverse the city using their character’s Esper abilities.

items.title: NTE: Neverness to Everness released on Steam - works on SteamOS / Linux with a workaround
items.summary: NTE: Neverness to Everness is the latest bigger Anime gacha game to arrive on Steam from Perfect World Games / Hotta Studio and it can work on SteamOS / Linux.
items.content:
NTE: Neverness to Everness is the latest bigger Anime gacha game to arrive on Steam from Perfect World Games / Hotta Studio and it can work on SteamOS / Linux.

Like many other Anime gacha games it uses Anti-Cheat Expert (ACE), which by default seems to have blocks in place but we can get it to work thanks to a community-made version of Proton.

You'll need the latest DW-Proton for it to work, otherwise with Valve's official Proton the game will just instantly quit after you hit the Play button.

After that, you'll be good to go and the game will launch properly. A bit of a nuisance, but this is the same story for multiple similar Anime games.

Hopefully this will get you up and running in no time! With DW-Proton it worked well for me on Fedora KDE.

Our anti-cheat page has been updated to note this status.

NTE: Neverness to Everness Release Date: 📅 7th July 2026 Platform: ⚛ Proton / Wine Devs: Hotta Studio , Perfect World Games Official links: Steam

items.title: NTE codes July 2026
items.summary: Our list of Neverness to Everness (NTE) codes and 1.2 livestream codes will help you claim free Annulith, which will help you roll for new S-Class characters.
items.content:
Image credit: Hotta Studio Guide by Dion Dassanayake Contributing Guides Writer Updated on July 8, 2026 Follow Neverness to Everness 8th July 2026 : We added new NTE codes that were released alongside version 1.2! Neverness to Everness codes will help you claim free Annulith so you can roll for new characters in the GTA-like gacha game. The free-to-play RPG is set in a Tokyo-like metropolis known as Hethereau, where supernatural events known as Anomalies occur.

While it's up to you to hunt down these Anomalies, the game is all about player freedom and there's tonnes of other things you can do including street races, taking on part-time jobs and buying your own luxury apartment. There's even a GTA-style wanted system and you can end up in jail! If you need a hand on your adventures, you can always use NTE codes to get rewards like free Annulith which will help you roll for new S-Class characters. Codes are shared on the official NTE Discord server , but to save you time we've rounded up the latest codes below including the NTE 1.2 livestream codes .

Here are all the working NTE codes as of 8th July 2026:

Think we can make our code pages better? Help us level up by taking our quick survey .

Neverness to Everness players could claim free rewards that were shared in the 1.2 livestream. These NTE livestream codes were only available for a limited amount of time, so you had to be quick in redeeming them!

They're expired now, but for reference, here's all the NTE 1.2 livestream codes:

The NTE 1.2 livestream codes, which were shared in an online broadcast that detailed the '999 Nights' update , were only available until Monday 29th June 2026 at 4.59pm BST.

Not sure how to redeem codes in Neverness to Everness? Here's what you need to do:

Run out of NTE codes to redeem? If you pre-registered for NTE then you'll have plenty of extra freebies waiting for you in your inbox.

Simply head to the 'Mail' section and find the message titled 'Pre-Reg Milestone Rewards' to claim the goodies. Here's everything you can get if you pre-registered:

Besides NTE codes, you can also get freebies for Neverness to Everness at launch by taking advantage of Twitch Drops. Twitch Drops let you claim rewards for NTE by watching participating streams.

If this sounds like something you're interested in, then here's what you need to do:

The number of rewards you get depends on how long you watch a participating stream. Here's the NTE rewards you can get and how long you need to watch a Twitch stream to get it:

Once you've unlocked a reward, you'll need to head to your Twitch Drops Inventory and claim it so you receive it in-game.

items.title: Neverness to Everness launches on Steam and Epic Games Store with Version 1.2 update
items.summary: Players can also claim a free exclusive in-game item as part of the game's platform expansion celebration.
items.content:
All News Neverness to Everness is now available on Steam and Epic Games Store (Image: Hotta Studio).

{" "} Neverness to Everness launches on Steam and Epic Games Store with Version 1.2 update .iframe-media {width: 100%;aspect-ratio: auto 16/9;} .img-media {width: 100%;aspect-ratio: auto 16/9;} .a-img-media {aspect-ratio: auto 16/9;} Players can also claim a free exclusive in-game item as part of the game's platform expansion celebration. Hotta Studio's Neverness to Everness is now officially available on the major PC platforms Steam and Epic Games Store, as well as Samsung's Galaxy Store on mobile platforms. NTE is also giving players a free exclusive in-game avatar frame to celebrate its platform expansion.

The game's arrival to Steam and the Epic Games Store also comes alongside the major Version 1.2 update, which introduces the new S-Class character Shinku, a new main story chapter, another exclusive vehicle obtainable through a draw event, and plenty of additional content.

Platforms: PC (via official launcher, Steam, and Epic Games Store), PlayStation 5, and mobile platforms (iOS and Android)

More than a month after its global launch, the supernatural gacha game, Neverness to Everness, has finally arrived on Steam and the Epic Games Store. It has also launched on the Galaxy Store, giving mobile players another option to download the game from. Previously, NTE was only available through its standalone PC launcher, PlayStation 5, and mobile’s App Store and Google Play.

To celebrate the expansion, Neverness to Everness offers the exclusive "Celebration Fireworks" in-game avatar frame for free. Players can grab the free item by redeeming the code WELCOMETONTE between 8 July and 18 August.

Once the redemption period ends, the code will no longer be valid. After redeeming it, be sure to claim the reward from your in-game Mail before it expires.

Yes, your progress in Neverness to Everness carries across all supported platforms, including Steam and Epic Games, provided you sign in with the same account using your linked email address and password. This means you can switch to Steam without losing your save data or starting over.

Sarah "KZ" Zulkiflee Sarah “KZ” Zulkiflee writes for GosuGamers, where she channels a long-standing obsession with Dota 2 into her work. While still often lost in the depths of MMR grinding, she has been actively experimenting with games across every genre imaginable. Six years into her career, she still wonders how she miraculously ended up in her dream industry. Anyway, she’s just happy to be here. items.title: S-Rank Character Xinku Deploys... 'Neverness to Everness' Version 1.2 Officially Launches
items.summary: Hotta Studio, a subsidiary of Perfect World, announced on the 8th that it has released a major 1.2 update for its supernatural urban open world RPG, 'Neverness to Everness' (NTE).T...
items.content:
Hotta Studio, a subsidiary of Perfect World, announced on the 8th that it has released a major 1.2 update for its supernatural urban open world RPG, 'Neverness to Everness' (NTE).

The 1.2 version, '999-Day Night Tale,' officially launched on the 8th, featuring the highly anticipated character Xinku, with Iloy also scheduled to appear sequentially. The update introduces a variety of new content, including 19 highly requested new costumes and the permanent board game mode, '999-Day Night Tale.

Alongside the update, the limited-time board 'Dawn's Break' for the S-Rank character Xinku has opened. Players can obtain a limited glider, limited livery, and a new costume for Xinku through this board. Additionally, a new fantasy-themed mount, 'Draco,' has been added, offering special synergy with the 'Dragon Hunter' costume.

Xinku, a member of the Paranormal Management Bureau's Containment Team 2, utilizes two distinct combat and movement modes. Upon entering the 'Crimson Flame Liberation' state, she switches to a new mode, dealing high-power Light-attribute ability damage with overwhelming force. She also possesses traits that increase movement speed and climbing speed in the open world, making exploration more convenient. Simultaneously, the limited research recruitment event 'Resolution' has begun, featuring an increased drop rate for the limited S-Rank Arc, 'Mirage of Red Flame.

The highlight of this update is the new permanent content, '999-Day Night Tale.' Featuring a structure where story and gameplay progress simultaneously, this narrative takes players—alongside Mint, Iloy, and Xinku—to the Warren Continent, a fantasy land created by a board game. Players will explore various regions, conquer monsters, and challenge the final boss, the Red Dragon. During the adventure, some equipment changes are reflected in real-time on the character's appearance, and a dyeing feature has been added to the corresponding costume series, allowing for personalized styling.

Furthermore, in the latter half of the version, players will be able to encounter the S-Rank character Iloy through the 'Lifeline' limited board. A variety of other content, including the 'Bang Bang Bang' auction event, the 'Thrilling Hide-and-Seek' event, and the new 'Fishing Operation' content, will be updated sequentially to provide users with a richer gameplay experience.

Meanwhile, following its availability on the official website, PlayStation 5, iOS, and Android, 'Neverness to Everness' officially launches on Steam, the Epic Games Store, and the Samsung Galaxy Store starting the 8th. More details can be found on the official website and the game lounge.

items.title: Neverness to Everness Shinku Build Guide: Best Arc, Cartridge, and Teams
items.summary: Maximize Shinku's Cosmos DPS in NTE with the best Arc, Cartridge, module stats, and team comps.
items.content:
Neverness to Everness has no shortage of strong DPS options, but Shinku stands out as one of the most rewarding on-field carries in the current roster. She hits hard, her animations are satisfying to watch, and she doesn't need energy to charge her Ultimate, which keeps her rotation clean and consistent. The catch? Shinku is more build-dependent than most DPS units in the game. Run her with the wrong Arc or skip the right Cartridge, and her damage falls off noticeably compared to other carries. Get the build right, and she's one of the strongest options available.

Here's the full picture at a glance before breaking down each piece:

The stat priorities here are non-negotiable if you want to see Shinku perform. Target 70% Crit Rate and 150% Crit DMG as your benchmarks. Once you've hit those numbers through sub-stats, you can start routing into Cosmos DMG for your main stat slot instead of stacking more Crit Rate.

Blushing Mirage is Shinku's signature Arc and her clear best-in-slot weapon. It provides a substantial ATK% buff alongside a Cosmos DMG multiplier, and it lets Shinku ignore 12% of enemy DEF, which is a meaningful damage boost against tanky targets. If you pulled Shinku, pulling her signature Arc is the most direct path to her damage ceiling.

For players skipping the weapon banner, here's how the alternatives stack up:

Camellia Society is the strongest option if you don't have the signature, followed by What's Desired . If you're working with A-class Arcs only, Fluff of Ferocity is currently the best pick for Shinku's build at that rarity tier.

warning Don't slot a generic ATK% Arc and expect full performance. Shinku's damage scaling is specific enough that the wrong weapon creates a noticeable gap compared to even her second-best alternatives. What Cartridge should Shinku use? Unlike her Arc slot where you have a few workable options, Shinku's Cartridge situation is straightforward: Lost Radiance is the only real choice. There's no close second.

Lost Radiance buffs Cosmos DMG and, after Shinku casts her Ultimate, lets her ignore 25% of enemy DEF. Pair that with the 12% DEF ignore from Blushing Mirage and you're stripping a significant chunk of enemy defenses every time her Ultimate fires. That DEF shred is a big part of why Shinku's damage spikes so hard at full build compared to partial setups.

For main stats on the Cartridge, prioritize Crit Rate first. If you've already hit the 70% Crit Rate target through your module sub-stats, swap to Cosmos DMG instead.

important Lost Radiance has no viable alternative for Shinku right now. Farming this Cartridge should be your first priority after pulling her. How should you set up Shinku's modules? The module layout matters as much as the stats themselves. For the Type III bonus module slot, always select Chaos DMG . This is fixed regardless of what other sub-stats you're chasing.

For sub-stats across all module slots, follow this priority order:

Shinku's kit rewards Crit stats more than most DPS units. The difference between hitting 70% Crit Rate and running at 50% is substantial enough that you should reroll sub-stats aggressively to hit the threshold before worrying about DMG% or Cycle Intensity.

Shinku's team requirements are fairly specific. Jiuyuan and Esper Zero are her two core supports, and she performs noticeably worse without both of them. The fourth slot is more flexible, but the best options aren't equal.

Hathor is the best fourth-slot option when available, making the first composition the strongest overall team. Sakiri and Hotori are solid alternatives, and Haniel works if you don't have access to the others.

Hotta Studio is launching the version 1.2 update for Neverness to Everness on July 8, 2026, titled " 999 Nights ." The patch is a milestone for the supernatural urban RPG, delivering a massive map expansion alongside the game's official arrival on Steam and the Epic Games Store.

PC players who prefer Steam can finally jump in with full cross-play and cross-progression active from day one. This allows appraisers to freely move their active game sessions between mobile, the standalone PC client, and the PlayStation 5 without losing progress.

The visual and narrative centerpiece of Version 1.2 is the addition of the Warren Continent , a sprawling new region that players can access by progressing through the main story episode titled "Fighting with a Dragon." The narrative drops the player and their colorful companions into a light-hearted, fantasy tabletop RPG-style adventure orchestrated entirely by the character Mint. This offers a more relaxed shift following the dramatic events of the prior patch's main crisis.

Two powerful S-Class characters are making their grand debut during this specific gacha update cycle. Shinku, a highly anticipated Cosmos Esper, headlines the first phase of the limited banner immediately following the conclusion of server maintenance. Hotta Studio has already confirmed direct day-one optimizations for Shinku, adjusting her passive abilities to stack outside of active combat for improved open-world utility alongside heavily refined target tracking for her ultimate ability. Following Shinku's banner window, the second phase will introduce Iroi, an S-Class Anima Esper, whose banner is scheduled to go live on July 29.

To celebrate the massive update and the concurrent Steam launch, the developers are distributing a free S-Class Standard Character Selection Box to any player who creates an account and unlocks the in-game mail system before the conclusion of the Version 1.2 patch cycle. Players will also receive a baseline of 600 Annulith in standard maintenance and bug fix compensation directly in their mailboxes. Additional customization systems arriving with the update include an outfit recoloring mechanic that lets players alter cosmetic palettes using newly introduced Dye Orbs. Maintenance will run from 06:00 to 11:00 UTC+8 on July 8, after which Hethereau opens up globally.

In gaming-related news, Bethesda has also detailed the global launch for DOOM: The Dark Ages – Revelations ... while Ubisoft confirmed the launch details for Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced .

items.title: Version 1.2 "999 Nights" Patch Notes
items.summary: Hey Appraiser, NTE will undergo version maintenance on July 8, 2026 from 06:00 to 11:00 (UTC+8). Once it's done, the all-new Version 1.2 "999 Nights" goes live. "Pink Paws Heist" will be temporarily unavailable starting at 05:30 (UTC+8) and
items.content:
Hey Appraiser,

NTE will undergo version maintenance on July 8, 2026 from 06:00 to 11:00 (UTC+8). Once it's done, the all-new Version 1.2 "999 Nights" goes live. "Pink Paws Heist" will be temporarily unavailable starting at 05:30 (UTC+8) and will reopen once maintenance wraps up.

Login will be unavailable during this period, so please plan accordingly. Since this update is on the larger side, we recommend using Wi-Fi or mobile data to complete the download. We apologize for any inconvenience!

※ The "Forsaken Path" Limited Board ends on July 8, 2026, at 05:59 (UTC+8). Be sure to head to the Fogshroud Perks page and redeem your rewards before then.

\

Create a character before Version 1.2 ends to receive an S-Class Standard Character Selection Box.

Duration: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Distribution: Sent via in-game mail after maintenance. Remember to claim the items before they expire.

※ Open the S-Class Standard Character Selection Box to choose any one of these characters to add to your roster: Sakiri, Baicang, Hathor, Fadia, Daffodill, or Jiuyuan.

\

Since launch, we have received invaluable feedback from our Appraisers to help improve the game. We sincerely apologize for any current shortcomings and are actively working to optimize the overall experience in upcoming updates.

Thank you for your patience and continued support. As a token of our gratitude, we are distributing the following gift:

● Appreciation Gift: Annulith x1,600

● Delivery: Sent via in-game mail after the July 8, 2026 update.

● Eligibility: Appraisers who create a character and unlock the "Mail" feature before Version 1.2 ends.

\

● Duration: July 8, 2026, 06:00–11:00 (UTC+8)

● Maintenance Compensation: Annulith x300

● Eligibility: Appraisers who created their character before July 8, 2026, 06:00 (UTC+8)

● Distribution: Sent via in-game mail after maintenance. Remember to claim the items before they expire.

※ Maintenance may end earlier or later than scheduled depending on actual progress. Please keep an eye on our official announcements for updates.

● Bug Fix Compensation: Annulith x300

● Eligibility: Appraisers who created their character before July 8, 2026, 06:00 (UTC+8)

● Distribution: Sent via in-game mail after maintenance. Remember to claim the items before they expire.

\

PC: Close the game, reopen the launcher, and click Update.

Android: Launch the game and follow the in-game update prompt.

iOS: Download or update from the App Store.

Mac: Download or update from the App Store.

PlayStation®: Open the system menu, select NTE, press the OPTIONS button on your controller, choose Confirm Upgrade, then select Check for Update.

\

1. New Character & Arc

● New Exclusive S-Class Character: Shinku!

Available: July 8 (after update) – July 29, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Unlock Requirement: Complete the main story quest "Prologue - A New Destination - Head to Eibon Antique Shop"

Board Details: The "Before the Dawn" Limited Board features the exclusive S-Class character Shinku, along with A-Class characters Mint, Edgar, and Adler.

During the event, the "Before the Dawn" Limited Board also features the "Overcast Canopy" glider livery, the "Hidden Dragon" Blizzard-V4 livery, and Shinku's outfit "Student of Terrasea". Claim them from the Resolve Perks page by accumulating 50/120/200 rolls, or obtain them randomly through rolls before reaching these milestones.

*For full details, head to Scarborough Fair in-game.

● New Limited S-Class Arc: "Blushing Mirage"

Available: July 8 (after update) – July 29, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Unlock Requirement: Complete the main story quest "Prologue - A New Destination - Head to Eibon Antique Shop"

Research Guide: Spend Tri-Keys or Resolve Keys to participate in the "Resolve Special" Arc Research Program and obtain the limited S-Class Arc "Blushing Mirage". This Arc is currently only available through this program.

*For full details, head to Arc Shop › Arc Research Program in-game.

2. New Story

● New Main Story Episode: "Fighting with a Dragon"

Ready to go? Our fantastical adventure is about to begin!

Available: After the Version 1.2 update

Unlock Requirement: Complete the main story episode "Dreamwalk Corridor" and reach Hunter Lvl 26.

※ The new location Warren Continent can be reached during the main story episode "Fighting with a Dragon".

※ In response to the concerns many Appraisers have shared about the continuation of the "Dreamwalk Corridor" storyline, we'd like to clarify: this main story episode is told out of chronological order, using a flashback structure. It depicts a lighter, more relaxed chapter set after the dust has settled on the "Scarlet Letter" incident, once everyone has returned to their peaceful daily lives. A new fantasy adventure is about to begin. Dive in and have a blast.

● City Hangouts

A new City Hangouts story featuring Shinku.

Available: After the Version 1.2 update

Unlock Requirement: Obtain the character Shinku, or complete the main story episode "Fighting with a Dragon".

● Home Time

A new Invitation mode featuring Shinku.

Available: After the Version 1.2 update

Unlock Requirement: Obtain the character Shinku and reach the required Bond Level.

3. New Location

● New Location: Warren Continent (reachable during the main story episode "Fighting with a Dragon")

A vast land brimming with fantasy, with adventures, challenges, and opportunities hiding in every corner. What lies at the next crossroads?

4. New Outfits & Vehicle Liveries

● Shinku's new outfit "Dragonhunter" is now available in the Shop

Discount Period: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Discount Price: Riftcrystal x2,680

Original Price: Riftcrystal x3,280 (18% off, limited time)

Shop Availability: Permanent

● Shinku's new outfit "Dawn Patrol" is now available in the Shop

Discount Period: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Discount Price: Riftcrystal x1,680

Original Price: Riftcrystal x2,480 (32% off, limited time)

Shop Availability: Permanent

● Shinku's new outfit "Student of Terrasea"

How to Get: While the "Before the Dawn" Limited Board is active, roll on the board and land on the matching tile to obtain the outfit, or claim it from the Resolve Perks page once you've accumulated enough rolls.

● Shinku's new outfit "Labyrinth Lily"

How to Get: Unlocked after you obtain Shinku and reach Bond Lvl 10.

● Shinku's new outfit "Normie Heroine"

How to Get: Purchased from the "999 Nights" Button Shop.

● The Porsche 918 Spyder exclusive liveries "Tomato Dash" and "Rose & Violet" are now available in the Shop

Available: After the July 8 version update (UTC+8)

Price: Riftcrystal x980 or Fons x3,000,000

*Purchase using either Riftcrystal or Fons; only one option may be selected.

5. New Mystery Box Event "Neon Rift" Goes Live

Duration: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: Spend Fons or Annulith to open "Neon Rift" Mystery Boxes. The cost goes up with each box you open. There are 15 boxes in total, and opening all of them unlocks every reward in the pool.

Mystery Box Contents: The "Neon Rift" Mystery Boxes feature the limited vehicle "Draco", Esper Zero's exclusive outfit "Glory Days", Esper Zero's exclusive outfit colors "Glory Days - Starfall" and "Glory Days - Braveheart", Fabricated Dice x10, A Handwritten Letter, Fons x100,000, and the exclusive avatar frame "Ember Trail".

6. New Events

● "Circle Gifts" Login Event

Duration: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: Log in for a total of 7 days to earn Solid Dice x10.

● "999 Nights" Limited-Time Event

Duration: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: The party's assembled! Ready to conquer all of Warren Continent! Take part during the event to earn rewards like Annulith, Fabricated Dice, Fons, Floe Cinema Tickets, Elite Hunter Guides, Chaotic Dye, and more.

● "Shadow-n-Seek" Limited-Time Event

Duration: July 17, 10:00 – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: One, two, three... don't move a muscle! During the event, take part to earn the event currency "Paper Cup Phone", then visit the Shadebound Shop to redeem rewards like the "Gotcha!" avatar frame, Annulith, Floe Cinema Tickets, Fons, and more.

● "Stamina Recharge" Limited-Time Event

Duration: July 13, 05:00 – July 20, 04:59 (server time)

Details: During the event, City Stamina is consumed at 2x efficiency in "Hethereau Hobbies" activities.

● "Gold Clash" Limited-Time Event

Duration: July 20, 05:00 – August 3, 04:59 (server time)

Details: During the event, earn Fons at 2x efficiency in "Pink Paws Heist".

7. Other Content

● "Eibon Partner Program" Now Live

Duration: July 8, 2026, 11:00 – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: During the event, Appraisers can earn rewards like Annulith by inviting Returning Appraisers back to the game.

Appraiser Share your invite code or invite link to bring back eligible Returning Appraisers, and earn base rewards plus Lucky Draw chances.

Once Returning Appraisers log in and finish the 3-day quests, they can also share their own invite code to bring other eligible Appraisers back and join the web event.

※ Returning Appraiser status can take a moment to update. If it hasn't refreshed, wait 2 to 4 minutes and reload the page.

● "999 Nights" Mini-Game

Available: After the Version 1.2 update

Details: Take part to earn "Mystery Buttons", then head to the Button Shop to redeem event rewards including Shinku's new outfit "Normie Heroine"; Esper Zero's outfits "Unassuming Warrior" and "The Blademaster"; Esper Zero's exclusive outfit colors "The Blademaster - Ocher", "The Blademaster - Morningstar", and "The Blademaster - Verdant"; Mint's outfits "Wild as the Wind", "Sylvan Hunter", and "Aurora Flame"; Mint's exclusive outfit colors "Sylvan Hunter - Ridge", "Sylvan Hunter - Lilac", "Sylvan Hunter - Bloomwhisper", "Aurora Flame - Shadeleaf", and "Aurora Flame - Dreammere"; Esper Zero's "Unassuming Warrior" dye orb set; Mint's "Wild as the Wind" dye orb set; the title "Dawn Warrior"; the namecard "Forever Fuzzy"; the avatar "Poet's Epilogue"; the avatar frame "Moonlit Dragon"; plus Annulith, Floe Cinema Tickets, Fons, and more.

● Version 1.2 "Circle Bounty" Guide

Available: July 8 (after update) – August 18, 23:59 (UTC+8)

Requirement: Complete the Main Quest "Relax Time"

Details: During the event, take on Circle Quests to earn Experience Points, raise your Circle Bounty Rank, and claim rewards like Fabricated Dice, Gold Carrota, De-noise Solution, A Handwritten Letter, and Expansion Cores.

Purchase Elite Hunter Supplies to unlock extra rewards as you rank up, including Annulith x680, Solid Dice x4, Fabricated Dice x2, Arc-light Trove x1, A Handwritten Letter x2, and Fons x700,000!

Purchase Honor Hunter Supplies to unlock all of the above rewards, plus the "Fuzzy" avatar, the "New Game+++" avatar frame, the "Summer Time" namecard, Fabricated Dice x5, A Handwritten Letter x1, and Fons x500,000!

\

◆ Add a teleport point at The Midnight Catzebo, where you can buy scratchers and scratch for prizes. Match the icons shown, and you win the prizes listed beneath them.

◆ Add an Outfit Recoloring feature for character outfits.

◆ Add Riichi Mahjong to Little Sparrow.

◆ Add a Fishkeeping feature.

◆ Add a Decor Blueprint feature.

◆ Add Bond quests for Shinku.

◆ Add Bond interactions for Shinku: reach the required Bond Level to unlock her passenger seat ride-along.

◆ Add text message conversations with Shinku.

◆ Add a combat tutorial for Shinku.

◆ Add Random Encounters for Iroi.

◆ Add the ability to display rare big fish on the back of your car.

◆ Add simplified controls for the Portable Player.

◆ Add a Player Reporting feature to Races - Online Battle mode.

◆ Add the ability to share Home Decor Blueprints.

◆ Raise the furniture Collection level cap and add one-tap collect. (If your Collection already went over the old cap before this update, the excess is now counted normally.)

◆ Improve Fight Club results scoring so knockouts and damage dealt count for more toward final rank and rewards.

◆ Add several new side quests.

◆ Reduce the difficulty of Anomaly Hunts at Appraisal Lvl 5 and above.

◆ Add long-press to bulk select upgrade materials when leveling up characters, Arcs, and Consoles.

◆ Add an option to hide parts and accessories on select outfits. (After this update, toggling Chaos's mask on or off moves from the G key to its own option in the Settings menu.)

◆ Convertible tops now stay down after your character gets out of the car.

◆ Add a semi-automatic throttle mode on mobile, toggleable in Settings.

◆ Add a Settings toggle for Arc special effects.

◆ Improve the elevator in the Pegasus Residence.

◆ Improvements to Bagel.

◆ Improve in-game avatar display.

◆ Improve controller support.

◆ Fix an issue where the quest log text could linger after completing Chaos's Encounter quest.

◆ Fix an issue where triggering an Anomaly Commission could break quest auto-tracking.

◆ Fix an issue where the new outfit notification dot could linger after switching your Appraiser's gender.

◆ Fix an issue where characters could get stuck at the table after leaving a Little Sparrow matchmaking room.

◆ Fix an issue where Hotori's Basic Attack button could disappear unexpectedly after casting her Ultimate.

◆ Fix an issue where the button to use the soap item fails to display properly in Fight Club when using a controller.

◆ Fix crashes that could occur in certain situations.

◆ Improve localization for several languages.

◆ Address various other known issues.

items.title: NTE is coming to Steam on July 8!
items.summary: Hey Appraisers, NTE officially launches on Steam on July 8. The Steam version supports cross-progression with existing platforms (mobile, PC, and more) across the Asia, America, Europe, and SEA servers, and launches straight into NTE's Vers
items.content:
Hey Appraisers,

NTE officially launches on Steam on July 8.

The Steam version supports cross-progression with existing platforms (mobile, PC, and more) across the Asia, America, Europe, and SEA servers, and launches straight into NTE's Version 1.2 content!

📺 Version 1.2 Preview Special Program

Follow this link to watch the livestream replay and learn about all the fun stuff coming in Version 1.2.

Wishlist it on Steam today, and start your Hethereau adventure on July 8!

items.title: Welcome to Hethereau, Appraiser.
items.summary: The NTE Steam page is now live. Wishlist now to stay updated on launch news.
items.content:
The NTE Steam page is now live.

Wishlist now to stay updated on launch news.

items.title: Neverness to Everness Won’t Stop at Launch: Hotta Studio Teases New Cities, Districts, and Nintendo Switch 2 Evaluation
items.summary: Hotta Studio says Neverness to Everness will expand after launch with new cities, story, and gameplay. Other consoles remain under evaluation.
items.content:
Hotta Studio , a subsidiary of Perfect World Games previously known for 2022's Tower of Fantasy , has an ambitious roadmap in mind for Neverness to Everness , and the team isn't treating the recent launch on PC, PS5, Android, and iOS as the finish line.

Set in the supernatural metropolis of Hethereau , the urban open world RPG powered by Unreal Engine 5 and enhanced with path tracing on PC is built around a simple but unusually broad fantasy: live in the city, explore its anomalies, fight when you need to, and fill the gaps between major story beats with the kind of everyday life sim details that help the world feel alive. That approach is already starting to take shape in post-launch support, with Perfect World Games recently rolling out the first time-limited board, Misty Tipsy Style , and introducing the new S-class character Hotori alongside fresh cosmetics and limited rewards.

In this exclusive interview with Wccftech, Hotta Studio hints at where it wants to take NTE next . The short version is that the studio plans to keep expanding Hethereau with new city districts, anomaly zones, gameplay systems, and main story chapters, while also teasing future travel to other cities and regions. It is a vision built around steady growth rather than a single content drop, and that applies to both world design and player support, from co-op and housing to performance optimization and platform availability, with the developers evaluating potential ports to other consoles.

If the team can deliver on that roadmap, Neverness to Everness could become more than just another gacha release and evolve into a long-running urban sandbox that keeps layering new things to do long after launch.

What is your main vision for the future of the game after launch, and where do you plan to take it?

Our core philosophy is to continuously deliver high-quality, player-centric content. Built around a supernatural urban open world, NTE will keep expanding its world depth, bringing fresh exploration, story, and gameplay experiences to players, aiming to become a long-lasting companion for everyone.

Will there be map expansions, new locations, and major gameplay additions in future updates?

Yes. We have a full long-term roadmap. We will keep launching new city districts, anomaly areas, major gameplay modes, and main story chapters, so that Hethereau continues to grow and always feels fresh and worth exploring.

Will there be an airport, or will there be the ability to travel to other cities/regions?

Specific details are still under wraps. We plan to gradually unlock exploration of other cities and regions through diverse map elements, making the overall world map even grander.

Will the game launch on Steam and/or the Epic Games Store? What about Nintendo Switch 2 and/or Xbox? Are you planning to support cloud gaming platforms like GeForce Now?

We’re aiming for a global release. Following PC, Steam, PlayStation 5 and mobile, other console platforms are under evaluation. GeForce Now will be supported in the open beta version. We want as many players to enjoy NTE as conveniently as possible.

What is the core selling point that sets Neverness to Everness apart from other gacha games? What is the main "hook"?

The greatest charm of NTE is its living, breathing supernatural metropolis. We blend open-world exploration, satisfying action combat, and urban life simulation. Players can explore, fight, buy homes, customize vehicles, and socialize — all for a one-of-a-kind immersive experience.
